footer.copy: A frontend-only platform for organizing lectures, workshops, roadmaps, community opportunities, and external learning links.

hero.title: One modern home for learning, workshops, and student growth.
hero.subtitle: Access department resources, follow career paths, register for workshops, and connect with the students building the technical community.

about.title: Designed for a growing academic and technical community
about.desc: The hub keeps learning material, events, links, and announcements in one clear place while staying lightweight and easy to manage.

mission.title: Mission
mission.desc: Help students learn faster through organized resources, peer-led workshops, and practical technical guidance.

vision.title: Vision
vision.desc: Build a collaborative department culture where every student can discover a path, practice skills, and contribute to real projects.

quick.title: Everything links to the tools students already use
quick.desc: Connect the frontend to Google Drive, Notion, Google Forms, recordings, and curated external resources.
quick.driveDesc: Lecture notes, sheets, summaries, and organized subject folders.
quick.notionDesc: Roadmaps, checklists, project ideas, and semester plans.
quick.formsDesc: Workshop registration, join requests, and feedback forms.
quick.recordingsDesc: Previous lectures and workshops for self-paced learning.

featured.title: Upcoming sessions students can act on
featured.desc: Highlight practical events, registration links, and recordings from previous sessions.

resources.title: Subject libraries built for quick study access
resources.desc: Each subject card can connect students to summaries, lecture notes, practice sheets, Drive folders, and trusted external resources.
resources.search: Search by subject or material type

algorithms.title: Algorithms
algorithms.desc: Sorting, graph algorithms, dynamic programming, complexity analysis, and practice problem sets.

dataStructures.title: Data Structures
dataStructures.desc: Arrays, linked lists, trees, heaps, hash tables, and implementation notes.

operatingSystems.title: Operating Systems
operatingSystems.desc: Processes, threads, memory management, file systems, and synchronization resources.

databases.title: Databases
databases.desc: SQL, normalization, ER diagrams, transactions, indexing, and project references.

programming.title: Programming
programming.desc: Foundations in C++, Java, Python, problem solving, and coding practice.

webDevelopment.title: Web Development
webDevelopment.desc: HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React, APIs, deployment, and frontend projects.

workshops.title: A calendar for practical learning
workshops.desc: Publish upcoming workshops, previous sessions, recordings, details, and registration links without needing a backend.

reactBootcamp.title: React Bootcamp
reactBootcamp.desc: Build a production-ready frontend with routing, components, state, and deployment.

cyberNight.title: Cybersecurity Night
cyberNight.desc: A beginner-friendly session on network security, Linux tools, and CTF practice.

databaseClinic.title: Database Clinic
databaseClinic.desc: Hands-on revision for SQL queries, normalization, indexes, and ER modeling.

aiIntro.title: AI & Data Science Intro
aiIntro.desc: A practical entry point into Python notebooks, datasets, and machine learning basics.

roadmaps.title: Clear paths from beginner to project-ready
roadmaps.desc: Guide students through technologies, practical milestones, and recommended external resources for each career direction.

web.title: Web Development
web.desc: Frontend fundamentals, React, APIs, accessibility, performance, and deployment.

cyber.title: Cybersecurity
cyber.desc: Networking, Linux, security basics, labs, CTFs, and defensive/offensive tracks.

ai.title: AI & Data Science
ai.desc: Python, statistics, data analysis, machine learning, and model deployment.

devops.title: DevOps
devops.desc: Linux, Git, containers, CI/CD, cloud platforms, monitoring, and automation.

game.title: Game Development
game.desc: Programming, engines, math, game loops, mechanics, testing, and publishing.

team.title: Students leading students
team.desc: Showcase department leaders and working groups with responsive cards that are easy to update.

community.title: More than files and links
community.desc: The platform can highlight opportunities, competitions, internships, and student achievements as the community grows.

opportunities.title: Opportunities
opportunities.desc: Scholarships, volunteering, open source, and campus programs.

competitions.title: Competitions
competitions.desc: Hackathons, ICPC practice, CTF challenges, and innovation contests.

internships.title: Internships
internships.desc: Curated internship links, preparation resources, and application deadlines.

achievements.title: Achievements
achievements.desc: Celebrate student projects, awards, publications, and team wins.

contact.title: Connect with the department community
contact.desc: Use social links and Google Forms to gather join requests, workshop feedback, and volunteer applications.

feedback.title: Share suggestions that improve the community
feedback.desc: Students can submit workshop ideas, resource requests, event feedback, and improvement suggestions through one simple Google Form.

faq.title: Common student questions
faq.desc: Keep the platform simple today while leaving room for future upgrades.

items.question: Does this website need a backend?
items.answer: No. Phase 1 is fully frontend-only and links to tools like Google Drive, Notion, Forms, and recordings.

items.question: Can the team update content on GitHub?
items.answer: Yes. Content is organized in reusable data and translation files so contributors can update cards, links, and copy safely.

items.question: Can this become a full platform later?
items.answer: Yes. Firebase, Supabase, authentication, uploads, comments, and dashboards can be added in later phases.
